description_long: This is a classic, upright fire hydrant painted in a bright red color, commonly found in urban environments for emergency water access. The hydrant features a domed top with a central operating nut, a cylindrical body, and three prominent hose connection outlets—two on the sides and one facing forward. The outlets are capped with hexagonal nuts, and the body is reinforced with flanged rings at the top and bottom for structural stability. The surface is smooth but shows some subtle casting lines and minor surface irregularities typical of cast iron hydrants. No chains or manufacturer markings are visible, and the hydrant appears robust and designed for permanent installation.
description: A bright red, cylindrical fire hydrant with three capped hose outlets and a domed top, featuring flanged rings and a sturdy, cast-iron appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main hose outlet and operating nut, with one side outlet visible.
description_view_1: Side view displaying both side outlets symmetrically, with the main outlet not visible.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the back outlet and one side outlet, with the domed top and flanges visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing both side outlets and the rear outlet, with the main outlet not visible.
